description: Stable anchor GCPs for Bahrain imagery (20th century) - manually refined coordinates
reference: Features that existed throughout 1960-2000 period, coordinates verified against modern satellite imagery

gcps.name: Bahrain Fort - NW Corner
gcps.description: Northwest corner of Bahrain Fort (Qal'at al-Bahrain), UNESCO World Heritage Site. Ancient fort with clear walls and corners visible in satellite imagery.
gcps.notes: Fort dates to 2300 BC, corner position stable

gcps.name: Adhari Pool
gcps.description: Adhari spring pool, natural feature with distinctive circular shape surrounded by vegetation

gcps.name: Ayn Al Hakim
gcps.description: Historic spring and water feature in central Bahrain, distinctive as oasis in desert.
gcps.notes: Spring may have varying visibility depending on season/water level

gcps.name: Manama Cemetery
gcps.description: Historic cemetery wall boundary in Hoora, Manama. Large rectangular walled area visible from satellite. Corner in south west of the graveyard

gcps.name: Arad Fort - NW Corner
gcps.description: Northwest corner of Arad Fort, 15th century coastal fort in Muharraq. Distinctive square fort with clear corners.

gcps.name: Casino Fountain - Muharraq
gcps.description: Historic fountain/water feature in Muharraq, distinctive circular feature in urban area.
gcps.notes: May not be visible in all historical imagery, urban development nearby

gcps.name: Jazirat Ash Shaykh
gcps.description: Small island south of main Bahrain island. Use mosque north on the island as reference
gcps.notes: Island shape stable, use centroid or distinctive cape

gcps.name: Ya'sub Island
gcps.description: Distinctive isolated island. West of Bahrain

gcps.name: Muhammediyya Island
gcps.description: Island north west of bahrain

gcps.name: Al Sayah Island
gcps.description: Island north of main Bahrain island, west of Muharraq. distinctive shape. Point on spring at center.

gcps.name: Um Al Na'san Farm - Path Intersection
gcps.description: Farm in the south of Um Al Na'san island, at the intersection of 2 dirt paths in the middle of the farm.

gcps.name: Riffa, second roundabout
gcps.description: Second roundabout in Riffa, central Bahrain. Distinctive road junction visible in satellite imagery.

gcps.name: Awali roundabout
gcps.description: Roundabout in Awali, the oil company town in central Bahrain. Distinctive circular road junction.
gcps.notes: Awali built in 1930s-40s for BAPCO oil workers

gcps.name: Sitra road bend
gcps.description: Distinctive bend in the road on Sitra island, east of main Bahrain island.

gcps.name: Fork in the road
gcps.description: Distinctive fork in the road in western Bahrain.

gcps.name: Underwater island in Fasht Al Adham
gcps.description: Submerged island feature within the Fasht Al Adham reef complex east of Bahrain. Distinctive shallow area visible in satellite imagery.
gcps.notes: Visibility depends on tide and image quality

gcps.name: Bend in the road in Hidd
gcps.description: Distinctive road bend in Hidd, eastern Muharraq. Visible road feature in satellite imagery.

gcps.name: Fork in the road, central
gcps.description: Fork in the road in central Bahrain, between Riffa and Sitra.

gcps.name: Fork in the road, west
gcps.description: Fork in the road in west-central Bahrain.

gcps.name: Oil tanks
gcps.description: Oil storage tanks near Sitra, distinctive circular tank farm visible in satellite imagery.
gcps.notes: BAPCO oil infrastructure, built mid-20th century

gcps.name: Dredge in reef
gcps.description: Dredged channel or feature in the reef far east of Bahrain. Distinctive man-made cut visible in satellite imagery.
gcps.notes: Man-made feature in natural reef, should be stable once created

gcps.name: Fork in road, oil wells north
gcps.description: Fork in the road near oil wells in southern Bahrain.

gcps.name: Fork in road, oil wells south
gcps.description: Fork in the road near oil wells in far southern Bahrain.

gcps.name: Island off fasht
gcps.description: Small island off the eastern fasht reef complex, southeast of Bahrain.

gcps.name: Fork in the road - center west
oil tanks in low sitra
gcps.description: Oil storage tanks in lower Sitra, east of main Bahrain island. Distinctive circular tank farm visible in satellite imagery.
gcps.notes: BAPCO oil infrastructure, built mid-20th century

gcps.name: Sanabis boys school
gcps.description: Sanabis Boys School in northern Manama. Distinctive building complex visible in satellite imagery.
